data.title: uniQure stock rockets 90% on FDA update for AMT-130
data.description:
UniQure (NASDAQ:QURE) stock shot up 90% Tuesday morning on news that the company had reached an agreement with the FDA on key elements for an accelerated approval pathway for its drug AMT-130 in the treatment of Huntington’s disease.

The Dutch biotech company said the FDA has agreed that data from ongoing Phase 1/2 studies compared to a natural history external control can serve as the primary basis for the drug’s Biologics License Application, which would avoid the need for an additional pre-submission study, according to a statement.

The FDA has also agreed that the composite Unified Huntington’s Disease Rating Scale can serve as an intermediate clinical endpoint for accelerated approval, uniQure (QURE) added.

AMT-130 received Regenerative Medicine Advance Therapy designation from the FDA in May 2024.

data.title: SSE outlines £22B investment plan to boost Scotland's grid
data.description:
U.K. utility SSE (OTCPK:SSEZF) (OTCPK:SSEZY) said Tuesday its power transmission business plans to spend at least £22B (~$28B) on grid infrastructure in Scotland over a five-year period beginning in April 2026.

The SSEN Transmission unit will submit its strategic investment plans that outline baseline spending of £6B, plus £16B allocated to pre-approved projects, to U.K. regulator Ofgem, the company said.

Expanding the grid and relevant infrastructure improvement is key to the U.K.'s green targets, allowing wind power from Scotland to be transported to demand centers in England.

SSEN Transmission's ability to deliver on its investment plan hinges on Ofgem approvals of the regulatory framework that supports the scale and speed of its critical projects, the company said.

SSE (OTCPK:SSEZF) (OTCPK:SSEZY) owns 75% of SSEN Transmission while the other 25% is owned by the Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan Board.

data.title: DocGo secures two-year transportation contract renewal with major Tennessee healthcare system
data.description:
• DocGo (NASDAQ:DCGO) announced that its Ambulnz subsidiary signed a two-year renewal of its contract with a major Tennessee healthcare system. 
  • The partnership currently supports approximately 17,000 trips annually, with plans to exceed 20,000 trips per year during this renewal period. 
  • DCGO shares were up over 2.9% on Tuesday.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Oracle skids, but Wall Street comes to defense as cloud growth continues
data.description:
Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) may have reported fiscal second-quarter results and guidance that were a bit weaker-than-expected, but Wall Street continued to praise the IT giant nonetheless, amid continued growth in the cloud.

Shares were down 8% in early trading, while other cloud software stocks were mostly higher, including Microsoft (MSFT), Adobe (ADBE), ServiceNow (NOW) and Salesforce (CRM).

"A fourth consecutive quarter of accelerating [current remaining performance obligations] growth to 20% y/y (vs. 18% last quarter) reinforces our confidence in a multi-quarter growth reacceleration that could evolve into a multi-year acceleration, underpinned by cloud and AI secular tailwinds, particularly as Meta joins companies like xAI, NVIDIA, and Cohere as customers leveraging Oracle's AI infrastructure," Piper Sandler analyst Brent Bracelin wrote in a note to clients.

"While the Oracle Cloud momentum was encouraging, consistent expense discipline coupled with improving margins across cloud applications and infrastructure was equally encouraging," Bracelin added. He raised his price target to $210 from $185 after the results and reiterated his Overweight rating.

Mizuho Securities analyst Siti Panigrahi also raised his price target (to $210 from $185) on Oracle, as he pointed out that the cloud strength is being driven by artificial intelligence.

"Oracle's broad portfolio of infrastructure and application products, its multi-cloud strategy through hyperscaler partnerships, and unprecedented AI demand position it well to reaccelerate growth," Panigrahi wrote in a note to clients. "We view the current share weakness as a buying opportunity."

KeyBanc Capital Markets analyst Jackson Ader noted that while the dip in the stock was the result of some "lofty expectations," bookings momentum is still on track and acceleration in cloud and software-as-a-service businesses are still expected to expand. "We still like Oracle heading into 2025," Ader wrote. He has an Overweight rating and $200 price target on the stock.

Morgan Stanley analyst Keith Weiss also upped his price target, but said with the negative bookings growth, focus is likely to turn back to the income statement. "With [free cash flow] increasingly pressured by rising capex, shares likely remain range bound as investors assess whether bookings momentum can return," Weiss wrote in a note to clients.

Looking to the next quarter, Oracle said it expects to earn between $1.50 and $1.54 per share on an adjusted basis (below the $1.57 per share estimate), with revenue growing between 7% and 9% year-over-year, or 9% to 11% in constant currency. It also remains very confident to see double-digit growth in full-year revenue, while cloud infrastructure should see 50% or more growth.

(This story has been updated with additional analyst commentary.)

data.title: J.P. Morgan initiates Stifel at Neutral on valuation
data.description:
J.P. Morgan started coverage of Stifel Financial (NYSE:SF) with a Neutral rating as the stock's recent rally appears to be pricing in "heightened expectations," creating a "difficult setup for near-term outperformance."

Stifel (NYSE:SF) is well-positioned to benefit from an improving investment banking outlook for 2025, wealth management should continue to push higher, and recruiting higher producing adviser teams should propel fee-based assets, Analyst Michael Cho said in a note to clients.

"We think Stifel could be entering a period of sustained revenues within Wealth (net interest income and balance sheet growth) while the Institutional segment benefits from higher investment banking activity levels, particularly within financials," Cho wrote.

"We like the secular drivers within wealth and think Stifel will be a clear beneficiary over time," he noted. However, the stock has risen 11% since the U.S. elections vs. the S&P 500's 5% increase, making further stock appreciation challenging.

J.P. Morgan's Neutral rating on Stifel (SF) aligns with the SA Quant rating and the average Wall Street rating, both at Hold.

data.title: AI language tutor startup Speak raises $78M at $1B valuation from OpenAI, others
data.description:
AI language tutor startup Speak has raised $78M in a new funding round, at a $1B valuation.

The Series C round was led by Accel with support from existing investors including the OpenAI Startup Fund, Khosla Ventures, and Y Combinator among others.

Speak said on Tuesday that the funding marked its second preempted raise this year, with the company's valuation doubling in the six months since its last round. The San Francisco-based startup has raised $162M to date.

"Speak has emerged as a standout player in consumer AI, demonstrating exceptional growth and market potential," said Ben Quazzo, partner at Accel.

Quazzo will join Speak's board.

Speak noted that this year it created over 25 million personalized lessons for its users, adapting to their pace and style of learning. Over the next year, Speak plans to become a dynamic learning experience powered by its underlying learning engine, the latest Large language models, or LLMs, and advancements in its speech technology.

Speak's Co-Founder and CEO Connor Zwick said that since the company's previous funding round, they have introduced an enterprise offering called Speak for Business to bring English learning to employers everywhere.

Generative AI services have become the talk of the town since the launch of Microsoft (MSFT)-backed OpenAI's ChatGPT in 2022. Globally, companies have launched their own LLMs, which can provide services such as content, image, video, and voice generation, to name a few.

data.title: Argentina ETF up 66%, surpassing all country ETFs by far
data.description:
Argentina ETF (NYSEARCA:ARGT) is by far the best performing country ETF in 2024 year-to-date.

Up 66%, it has surpassed the U.S. (SPY), China (MCHI), and any other country ETF.

Bespoke Investment Group posted in X.com that “investors seem to like the job [President Javier Milei] has done with the country.”

data.title: Trinseo signs agreement to extend debt maturities; shares up
data.description:
• Trinseo (NYSE:TSE) and its subsidiaries have signed a transaction support agreement with holders and lenders of its senior notes and term loans.
  • The transactions contemplated by the agreement include refinancing $115M of 2025 senior notes with $115M in 2028 refinance term loans, on similar terms to the September 2023 credit facility.
  • The company will secure a new $300M revolving credit facility, maturing in February 2028, to replace the existing facility due in May 2026.
  • The transaction will also include exchanging at least $330M of 2029 senior notes for new 2029 second lien senior secured notes at a discount to par, capturing at least $49M in discount value.
  • Upon consummation of the transactions contemplated by the agreement, the company will have no debt scheduled to mature until 2028.
  • The company plans to launch an exchange offer, offering holders of its 5.125% senior notes due 2029 the chance to exchange them for new 7.625% second lien senior secured notes due 2029, at a discount to the existing notes' par value.
  • The transactions, including the exchange offer, are expected to close in January 2025.
  • TSE shares were up 25.6% on Tuesday.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CervoMed stock crashes 77% on failed study for dementia drug
data.description:
• Shares of CervoMed (NASDAQ:CRVO) plunged 77% Tuesday after the company said a Phase 2b study of its drug candidate neflamapimod for the treatment of dementia with Lewy bodies failed to meet its primary or key secondary endpoints.
  • CervoMed (CRVO) said the full data set from the study is expected to be available in January, with data from the initial 16 weeks of the open label extension portion of the study available in late Q2 2025, according to a statement.
  • Shares of CervoMed (CRVO) gained last month on news the drug had been granted Orphan Drug Designation by the FDA for frontotemporal dementia.

data.title: Hydro One subsidiary prices offering of $750M medium term notes
data.description:
• Hydro One Limited (TSX:H:CA) announced that its subsidiary, Hydro One, has priced a $750M medium term notes offering, including $375M of 4.46% notes due 2053 (Series 55) and $375M of 4.25% notes due 2035 (Series 60).
  • The additional Series 55 Notes will be issued at a price of $101.560 per $100.00 principal amount plus accrued interest, for a re-opening yield of 4.362%, and the additional Series 60 Notes will be issued at a price of $102.522 per $100.00 principal amount plus accrued interest, for a re-opening yield of 3.938%.
  • The net proceeds from the issuance of the additional notes will be ~$762M.
  • The offering is expected to close on December 11, 2024.
  • Upon completion of the offering, there will be $675M aggregate principal amount of Series 55 notes outstanding and $1.075B aggregate principal amount of Series 60 Notes outstanding.
  • Source: Press Release
